body:
9.40 THESIS -- PART 1 of 3 (Sections 1-2). Grounded in actual code inspection, not generic ML theory. Where I don't have full certainty on a phase's exact internals I say so rather than fabricate specifics.

=== SECTION 1: WHAT EACH PHASE LEARNS, FROM WHERE, AND WHAT IT'S MISSING ===

Phase 1 (stratified baseline): builds g_HourlyWR[24] and day-of-week win rate stats. Information source: this instrument's own price history, sliced by hour-of-day and weekday, on the ONE chart timeframe the EA is attached to. Method: straightforward historical frequency counting.

Phase 2 (9-regime IC): computes information coefficients per signal component (momentum, reversal, volatility, bias, pattern, predictive) PER REGIME, on the same single instrument/timeframe. This is the richest phase -- it's where the system learns which of its 6 signal layers actually correlates with outcomes, conditioned on regime. Feeds Phase 3's weight search as a warm-start.

Phase 4 (6D ProbDB): a 25,000-slot table (regime x scoreRange x pattern x session x volatility -> observed win probability), populated by linear-scan-and-replace-least-observed. Pure single-instrument, single-timeframe frequency counting across 5 categorical buckets -- no continuous interpolation between buckets, no cross-timeframe or cross-instrument input.

Phase 5/6 (ghost validation): simulates trades against history using the EA's own live decision logic, gates on win rate/profit factor/expected value/consecutive-loss/statistical-significance/walk-forward-stability (6 gates, need 3+ met). This is the actual go-live gate.

WHAT'S NOT BEING LEARNED: every phase operates on ONE symbol's ONE timeframe. The only cross-instrument structure I found anywhere in the file is the Layer 2 'MultiAssetIntelligence' struct (currency strength decomposition, cross-pair validation symbols) -- but it reads live, it isn't wired into any calibration PHASE. None of Phase 1/2/4/5/6 learn anything from correlated pairs, higher timeframes as a distinct data source (vs. just the live MTF momentum check I found earlier in OnTick), or cross-asset context (gold/SPX/oil, which a 'LEGACY MULTI-ASSET' comment I saw early in the file suggests used to exist and may have been deprioritized).

IDEAL INFORMATION DIET: Phase 2 (IC per regime) is exactly the right idea -- decompose 'does this signal work' by context rather than treating the whole history as one bucket. The gap is that 'context' currently means regime+session+volatility only. A genuinely richer context would ALSO include: the higher-timeframe regime (is H1 trending while M15 ranges -- that's a real, different regime than either alone), and correlated-pair confirmation (the currency-strength data already computed live but never fed back into calibration). Phase 4's 6D table is the natural place to add a 7th dimension (higher-TF regime agreement: aligned/opposed/neutral) rather than building a parallel structure.

HOW PHASES SHOULD RELATE: currently they're mostly sequential handoffs (Phase 2 seeds Phase 3, Phase 3 feeds Phase 4/5/6) -- that's reasonable. What's missing is a feedback path FROM live trading BACK to recalibrating which regime/session/pattern buckets deserve more or less trust -- CATREC (the failure-diagnosis system I saw in OnTick) is the closest thing to this, but it reacts to failure after the fact rather than continuously updating confidence the way Phase 2's IC computation does during calibration.

=== SECTION 2: CORRELATION AND RELATIONSHIP ARCHITECTURE ===

WHAT'S MODELED NOW: regime-conditional signal correlation (Phase 2 IC), a live (not calibration-integrated) currency-strength cross-pair confirmation layer, live multi-timeframe momentum convergence (3 timeframes, momentum-only, live-only per OnTick's MTF check), and regime transition probabilities (g_RegimeTransitions[from][to] matrix, tracked but I did not find it actually being READ anywhere to influence a live decision -- worth confirming in implementation).

WHAT IT'S BLIND TO: (1) whether the SAME regime classification on a higher timeframe agrees or conflicts with the current timeframe's regime -- this is a genuinely different, valuable relationship an EA with no external data feed CAN compute cheaply (iADX/iRSI/iMA calls on a higher PERIOD_ constant cost nothing extra); (2) whether the regime-transition matrix's LEARNED transition probabilities are ever used to anticipate a regime change before hysteresis confirms it, rather than only reacting after confirmation; (3) whether currency-strength divergence (already computed) actually correlates with THIS regime's historical win rate -- it's live but not calibration-validated the way every other signal component is via Phase 2 IC.

WHAT A GOOD ARCHITECTURE LOOKS LIKE FOR THIS EA SPECIFICALLY: not generic multi-asset ML -- concretely, for limited compute and no external feeds, the highest-value additions are (a) higher-timeframe regime agreement as a 7th Phase-4 dimension (cheap: reuses ClassifyBarToRegime_WithHysteresis on H1/H4 data that's already loaded), and (b) running the EXISTING currency-strength/cross-pair signal through the SAME Phase 2 IC process the other 6 components get, so it earns or loses trust the same principled way instead of being a fixed-weight bolt-on. Representation: extend ComponentWeights-style per-regime weighting to this signal rather than inventing a new mechanism. Consumption: same as everything else -- feeds CalculateStatisticalEdge's weighted blend.

(Part 2/3 covers the methodology audit synthesis and the exponential-improvement case. Part 3 covers the prioritised implementation plan.)
